autograd package#

autograd.context module#

class autograd.context.Context[source]#

Bases: object

Context passed to forward to save variables for backward.

save_for_backward(*tensors)[source]#

Save tensors for backward.

property saved_tensors#

Access saved tensors for instance of Context.

class autograd.ops.VariableSum[source]#

Bases: Function

static backward(ctx, grad_output)[source]#
static forward(ctx, a, dim=None, keepdims=False)[source]#
Parameters:

  • a – np.ndarray

  • dim – int or tuple of ints or None

  • keepdims – if True, result shape matches ‘a’ in rank (PyTorch-style)

autograd.variable module#

class autograd.variable.Variable(data: int | float | list | tuple | ndarray | Variable, requires_grad: bool = False, grad_fn: Function | None = None, is_leaf: bool = True)[source]#

Bases: object

property T: Variable#
add_(other)[source]#
backward(gradient: ndarray | None = None)[source]#

Compute gradients of the graph w.r.t current tensor. If gradient = None, then its assumed to be one.

For custom gradients, ensure that: gradient.shape == tensor.shape

Parameters:

gradient (Optional[np.ndarray]) – gradient of tensor

property data: ndarray#
detach() → Variable[source]#
property dtype: dtype#
exp() → Variable[source]#
log() → Variable[source]#
mean(dim: int | tuple | None = None, keepdims: bool = False) → Variable[source]#
mul_(other)[source]#
property ndim: int#
numpy() → ndarray[source]#

convert into numpy.ndarray

register_hook(fn: Callable[[Variable], None])[source]#
requires_grad_(val=True) → None[source]#

In-place requires-grad change

reshape(shape: tuple) → Variable[source]#
property shape: Tuple#
squeeze(dim: int | None = None, in_place: bool = True) → Variable | None[source]#

removes dims with no entries :param dim: dimention to squeeze (if none, all dims that can be squeezed will be squeezed) :param in_place: returns new instace (squeezed) if False, modifies current instance if True

sum(dim: int | tuple | None = None, keepdims: bool = False) → Variable[source]#
tolist() → List[source]#

convert into python-list

zero_()[source]#
zero_grad()[source]#
autograd.variable.add(a, b)[source]#
autograd.variable.div(a, b)[source]#
autograd.variable.enforce_tensor(x) → Variable[source]#

Converts ‘x’ into a Tensor instance if not already. Used for tensor-ops.

Compute gradients of the graph w.r.t current tensor. If gradient = None, then its assumed to be one.

For custom gradients, ensure that: gradient.shape == tensor.shape

Parameters:

gradient (Optional[np.ndarray]) – gradient of tensor
